title: Password and credential handling
objective: Add password hashing and credential validation utilities without adding login endpoints yet.
acceptance: Password hashing utility exists and uses the existing passlib bcrypt dependency.
Password verification utility exists and is covered by tests.
Credential helpers are typed and reusable by future auth endpoints.
No plaintext password is stored or logged.
No login/logout endpoint is added in this task.

title: Login/logout/me API
objective: Add authentication endpoints for login, logout, and current user using the established User model and credential helpers.
acceptance: Login endpoint validates user credentials.
Current user endpoint returns the authenticated user.
Logout behavior is explicitly implemented or documented depending on the chosen session/token strategy.
Inactive users cannot authenticate.
Tests cover success, bad password, unknown user, inactive user, and current user behavior.

title: Role-based access control
objective: Add role-based access control primitives for admin, optician, staff, and readonly users.
acceptance: Reusable role-checking dependency or helper exists.
Supported roles are admin, optician, staff, and readonly.
Role checks are tested without protecting Patient endpoints yet.
The implementation is compatible with FastAPI dependency injection.
Unauthorized and forbidden cases are distinguishable.

title: Protect Patient API with auth
objective: Require authenticated access and appropriate roles for Patient API endpoints.
acceptance: Patient endpoints reject unauthenticated requests.
Readonly users can read but cannot mutate Patient resources.
Authorized staff roles can create/update/delete according to the agreed role policy.
Tests cover unauthenticated, readonly, and authorized access.
Existing patient behavior remains intact for authorized users.

title: Standard API error contract
objective: Define and apply a consistent API error response contract for backend endpoints.
acceptance: A standard error schema or documented error shape exists.
Validation, not found, unauthorized, forbidden, and unexpected error cases are considered.
Tests cover representative error responses.
The implementation does not leak sensitive internal details.
Existing tests are updated intentionally where the response contract changes.

title: Typed API client
objective: Add a typed frontend API client for backend integration.
acceptance: A typed API client exists for core auth and patient endpoints.
API base URL configuration is environment-driven.
Patient list response type supports items, total, offset, and limit.
Errors are surfaced in a predictable frontend-friendly format.
Build and type checks pass.

title: Login/logout frontend
objective: Implement frontend authentication screens and session handling.
acceptance: Login screen exists and submits credentials through the API client.
Logout flow clears local session state.
Current user/session state is represented in the UI shell.
Authentication errors are displayed without leaking sensitive details.
Build and available frontend checks pass.

title: Patient list page
objective: Implement patient list, search, filters, and pagination UI.
acceptance: Patient list page displays API results.
Search and pagination controls exist.
Loading, empty, and error states are handled.
The page uses the typed API client.
Build and available frontend checks pass.

title: Patient detail page
objective: Implement patient detail view.
acceptance: Patient detail page fetches and displays one patient.
Missing patient and API error states are handled.
Navigation from list to detail is supported when list page exists.
The page uses the typed API client.
Build and available frontend checks pass.

title: Patient create form
objective: Implement patient creation form with validation.
acceptance: Patient creation form includes required fields.
Client-side validation mirrors the backend schema where practical.
Successful create navigates or displays the created patient.
API validation errors are displayed clearly.
Build and available frontend checks pass.

title: Patient edit form
objective: Implement patient editing form with validation.
acceptance: Patient edit form loads existing values.
Partial update is sent through the API client.
Validation and API errors are displayed clearly.
Successful update refreshes or navigates to the detail view.
Build and available frontend checks pass.

title: Patient archive/delete flow
objective: Implement controlled patient archive or delete workflow in the frontend.
acceptance: A guarded delete or archive action exists from the patient detail context.
A confirmation step is required before destructive action.
Successful deletion/archive updates navigation or UI state.
Failure states are displayed clearly.
No irreversible UX shortcut is introduced.

title: Frontend smoke tests
objective: Add smoke tests for the core frontend patient flow.
acceptance: Smoke test tooling is documented and runnable.
At least login shell, patient list route, and patient detail route are covered when available.
Tests avoid relying on production services.
CI or local validation command is documented.
Build and available test commands pass.

title: Automated encrypted backups
objective: Define and implement the first automated encrypted PostgreSQL backup approach or, if infrastructure is not available, add a precise runbook and script skeleton.
acceptance: Backup strategy is documented.
Encryption approach is documented without storing real secrets.
Retention and storage assumptions are explicit.
A runnable script or clearly marked script skeleton exists when feasible.
Restore verification is deferred to TASK-089.

title: Restore drill
objective: Document and script a restore drill for PostgreSQL backups.
acceptance: Restore drill checklist exists.
Restore target environment assumptions are explicit.
Verification steps after restore are documented.
No production destructive command is run automatically.
Secrets are never committed.

title: Health checks
objective: Add application health checks for app, database, Redis, and version visibility.
acceptance: A lightweight health endpoint exists.
Database and Redis checks are represented appropriately.
The endpoint does not expose secrets.
Tests cover healthy and representative unhealthy states where practical.
Docker or deployment integration is documented if changed.

title: Structured logging
objective: Introduce structured backend logging without logging sensitive patient data.
acceptance: Backend logging format is documented.
Request or application logs are structured enough for operations.
Sensitive patient data, tokens, and passwords are not logged.
Existing LOG_LEVEL behavior remains compatible.
Tests or documentation cover the logging policy.

title: Monitoring and alerts
objective: Document monitoring and alerting expectations for production.
acceptance: Monitoring targets are listed: uptime, disk, database, backend errors, backup status.
Alert severity levels are defined.
Initial manual or external monitoring approach is documented.
No external secrets are committed.
Follow-up implementation gaps are explicit.

title: Admin documentation
objective: Add administrator documentation for deployment, accounts, operations, backups, and incident basics.
acceptance: Admin guide exists.
Account and role management is described based on current implementation.
Backup, restore, deployment, and incident references are linked when available.
No secrets or environment-specific credentials are committed.
Known gaps are listed.

title: User documentation
objective: Add end-user documentation for the available application workflows.
acceptance: User guide structure exists.
Patient workflows are documented when available.
Screens or routes are described without inventing unavailable features.
Known incomplete areas are marked clearly.
No sensitive sample data is used.

title: End-to-end critical path tests
objective: Add end-to-end tests for the critical application path when frontend and auth are available.
acceptance: E2E tooling and command are documented.
Critical path includes login and basic patient flow when implemented.
Tests use non-production test data.
CI integration is documented or implemented if practical.
Limitations are explicit if the frontend is not ready.

title: User acceptance testing
objective: Prepare user acceptance test scenarios for production readiness.
acceptance: UAT scenario document exists.
Patient, auth, prescription, order, and operations scenarios are represented as applicable.
Each scenario has preconditions, steps, expected result, and pass/fail fields.
Unavailable features are marked as future or blocked.
No real patient data is included.

title: Production launch
objective: Prepare the production launch checklist and go/no-go decision template.
acceptance: Go/no-go checklist exists.
Launch communication, validation, rollback, and monitoring steps are included.
Required dependencies are explicitly listed.
No production deployment is triggered automatically.
Post-launch checks are documented.

title: Post-launch stabilization
objective: Prepare the post-launch stabilization process and issue triage policy.
acceptance: Post-launch stabilization checklist exists.
Bug severity and triage rules are defined.
Daily review and rollback criteria are documented.
Known support channels and escalation paths are placeholders, not invented secrets.
No runtime behavior is changed.
